frontmatter.Parallel L1 file: *not yet run* — L1 Scite mission for Era 50 pending
frontmatter.Tradition-period: Generative AI / postdigital / LLM era (GPT-3 through current frontier models)
frontmatter.IMPORTANT: This is the era that the entire VALOR corpus was written TO ANALYSE.

education.coreQuestion:
**What does the emergence of generative AI mean for education, and which capacities
— historically identified as prerequisites for independent adult judgment — are at
substitution risk?**

Unlike prior eras, this question is not asked retrospectively. It is the live
question the VALOR corpus was built to answer, using the preceding 5,000 years of
cross-civilisational evidence as the interpretive framework.

capacities.name: Foundational cognition
capacities.evidence: Writing, arithmetic, memorisation all substitutable by AI; Kosmyna 2025 MIT study shows reduced brain engagement during AI-assisted essays

capacities.name: Practical judgment (*phronesis*)
capacities.evidence: Without the formation practice — confronting uncertainty, weighing particulars — the capacity is not built; Klein & Klein 2025 "Sovereignty Trap"

capacities.name: Ethical formation
capacities.evidence: Moral reasoning can be outsourced to AI-generated argumentation; Elish 2019 "moral crumple zone" describes liability displacement

capacities.name: Communicative competence
capacities.evidence: The cognitive discipline writing provides (structuring thought) can be bypassed; the performance remains

capacities.name: Embodied knowledge
capacities.evidence: AI cannot substitute for embodied skill acquisition (physical crafts, sports, music performance)

capacities.name: Critical evaluation
capacities.evidence: Lee et al. 2025 (CHI): self-reported reduction in cognitive effort when AI is available; Bastani et al. 2025 PNAS: guardrail-free AI harms learning

capacities.name: Self-knowledge
capacities.evidence: AI can simulate reflection but cannot substitute for lived introspection

constants.name: Unity of knowledge and virtue
constants.state: Severed by default — outputs now routinely produced without the formation that historically grounded them

constants.name: Self-regulation
constants.state: Eroded — "desirable difficulties" (Bjork) are optional when AI provides frictionless alternatives

constants.name: Communicative competence
constants.state: Severed — the discipline of composition can be bypassed; the artefact remains

constants.name: Irreducibility of *phronesis
constants.state: Validated NEGATIVELY — the AI era reveals, by contrast, what phronesis actually is: a capacity that develops only through practice AI removes

constants.name: Protective self-knowledge
constants.state: Threatened — unclear whether people will develop accurate self-assessment in AI-mediated environments

institutions: **Scaffolded AI use** (structured guardrails, explicit formation first)
Kestin et al. 2025 Harvard physics RCT; MIT Mentor interventions
Two-week evidence favourable; no long-term retention data
Klein & Klein 2025 "Sovereignty Trap"; Lin et al. 2026 "Cognitive Integrity Threshold" (ICML)
Bastani et al. 2025 PNAS (unguardrailed AI harms); Barcaui 2025 (post-removal retention drops)
UNESCO 2021 "AI and Education" (precautionary principle); various K-12 policies
Policy fragmented — NCES: only 31% of US public schools had a written AI policy as of Dec 2024
canonicalTexts: Kosmyna et al. 2025, "Your Brain on ChatGPT" (MIT)
Bastani et al. 2025 PNAS, "Generative AI without Guardrails Can Harm Learning"
Kestin et al. 2025 *Scientific Reports*, "AI Tutoring Outperforms Active Learning"
Lee et al. 2025 CHI, "AI Critical Thinking"
Ma et al. 2025 JCAL, "Meta-Analysis AI Learning Outcomes"
NCES / Child Trends 2025, "Most Public Schools Lack AI Policies"
stageMapping: AI assistants increasingly available; 31% of US schools have written AI policies
Foundational cognition formation at risk before it occurs
Widespread ChatGPT use for writing assignments
Critical formation period; writing-as-formation potentially bypassed
AI-assisted technical training; vibe coding in industry
Most sources on AI-and-learning concern this stage
Debate: does AI free doctoral researchers for original insight, or bypass the formation that produces such insight?

centralDebate.knowledge: AI extends human capability; minimum essential knowledge reduced to what AI cannot do
centralDebate.aiQuestion: Kestin 2025 (short-term learning gains); general productivity literature

centralDebate.knowledge: AI substitutes for capacities people need to develop personally; the shift severs output from formation
centralDebate.aiQuestion: Kosmyna 2025; Bastani 2025; Klein & Klein 2025 Sovereignty Trap

centralDebate.knowledge: AI permitted only after foundational formation; or scaffolded from the start with explicit guardrails
centralDebate.aiQuestion: Kestin 2025 + prior formation; Bjork's "desirable difficulties" preserved

centralDebate.knowledge: Until empirical evidence clarifies long-term effects, AI should be restricted in formative educational contexts
centralDebate.aiQuestion: UNESCO 2021; Jonas-style precaution given catastrophic + irreversible + systemic risk

valorFindings: **The Cognitive Threshold framework**: minimum formation level below which AI assistance produces dependency rather than extension. This is the paper's central constructive claim.

**The Liability Sponge / Moral Crumple Zone**: humans ultimately absorb liability for AI outputs they cannot independently validate. Elish 2019 framed this in human-robot contexts; VALOR applies it to AI-assisted knowledge work.

**The vibe-coding phenomenon** is an unusually clean test case: software developers producing AI-generated code they cannot read or validate. This structurally replicates what the historical record consistently identifies as dangerous — a person professionally responsible for outputs they cannot evaluate.

**The RCT evidence converges**: Bastani 2025 (unguardrailed AI harms learning), Barcaui 2025 (post-removal retention drops), Kosmyna 2025 (reduced brain engagement), Lee 2025 (self-reported reduced cognitive effort) all point in the same direction. Kestin 2025 is the main counter-evidence and tests *scaffolded* AI, not substitutive AI — so it does not refute the scaffolded- formation position; it supports it.

**Policy is absent at scale**: NCES December 2024 data show 31% of US public schools had a written AI policy. The institutional substrate for the emerging pedagogical response does not yet exist at scale.
education.aiEraImplication:
For every earlier era (14, 16, 25, etc.) the "AI-era implication" section maps the
era's insight onto the current question. For Era 50 itself, the question is reversed:
**the 5,000-year cross-civilisational consensus becomes the interpretive framework
for analysing the current moment**.

VALOR's manuscript is this analysis. Its central conclusion: the AI era produces the
first sustained technological pressure on formation itself, not merely on outputs.
The response — if one is to be adequate — must reinstate formation as the primary
educational goal, with AI integrated only above the Cognitive Threshold.
education.crossRefL1:
L1 file not yet run (Era 50 is in Batch 5, eras 41–50, pending). When run, L1 should
cover: LLM model releases (GPT-3 2020, GPT-4 2023, GPT-5 2026), EU AI Act, US
executive orders, China's AI governance approach, the OpenAI / Anthropic / Google
DeepMind / Meta competitive dynamics, compute-access politics.

L1 ↔ L2 integration will be rich: who builds AI (L1) determines what AI does to
formation (L2). The VALOR manuscript's policy section is fundamentally an L1↔L2
integration argument (governance must take formation as the primary concern).
education.verification:
All modern citations: verified via VALOR 5-phase verification pipeline (2026-03-2x).
0 fabricated citations, 3 fabricated statistics caught and corrected, 1 fabricated
citation caught and removed. Every DOI resolves; every URL was checked. Classical
references (to the constants identified in Eras 01–47) inherit their verification
from those eras.
